sourceMovie.title: The Man Who Wasn't There
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Man Who Wasn't There (2001) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Hustle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Man Who Wasn't There clicked for you, American Hustle (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Gangster Squad
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Man Who Wasn't There clicked for you, Gangster Squad (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: I Love You Phillip Morris
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Man Who Wasn't There clicked for you, I Love You Phillip Morris (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Man Who Wasn't There?
faq.answer: Try American Hustle, Gangster Squad, I Love You Phillip Morris—each shares DNA with The Man Who Wasn't There while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Man Who Wasn't There?
faq.answer: The Man Who Wasn't There (2001) is best described as Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Man Who Wasn't There worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
